scenarios.title: Agent download prompt does not appear after login
scenarios.subtitle: install or migration — same symptom, same first checks.
scenarios.data_search: agent download prompt does not appear after login install or migration same symptom same first checks network tech logs in to rapidreconciler getgsi com with gsiadmin credentials but no agent download prompt is shown this affects both fresh installs and migrations to a new server the underlying causes overlap with one extra check for migrations existing agent registration on the old server interfering network tech logs in to rapidreconciler getgsi com with gsiadmin credentials but no agent download prompt is shown the page loads normally but the agent download ui is missing install migration fresh install no prior agent registration for this customer migration to a new server existing agent registration on the old server may interfere verify setup step is created in valc first check for both install and migration the agent download prompt only renders when the customer s setup step is set to created on the client details tab in valc any other value will hide the prompt in valc open manage client client details tab for the customer verify setup step is set to created if not change it to created and save have the customer hard-refresh the browser ctrl f5 cmd shift r and log in again with the gsiadmin credentials if the prompt still does not appear check that no other browser session has the customer logged in elsewhere clear existing agent registration on the old server migration only specific to migrations if the customer is migrating to a new server the agent registration on the old server may still be active and the prompt will not appear on the new server until that registration is properly handled in valc open manage client client details for the migrating customer change setup step to created and save hard-refresh the browser on the new server ctrl f5 and log in again with gsiadmin credentials the agent download prompt should now appear

solutions.title: Verify Setup Step is "Created" in VALC
solutions.when_applies_html: First check for both install and migration. The agent download prompt only renders when the customer's Setup Step is set to "Created" on the Client Details tab in VALC. Any other value will hide the prompt.
steps_html: In VALC, open Manage Client → Client Details tab for the customer.
Verify Setup Step is set to "Created". If not, change it to "Created" and save.
Have the customer hard-refresh the browser (Ctrl+F5 / Cmd+Shift+R) and log in again with the GSIADMIN credentials.
If the prompt still does not appear, check that no other browser session has the customer logged in elsewhere.

solutions.title: Clear existing agent registration on the old server (migration only)
solutions.when_applies_html: Specific to migrations. If the customer is migrating to a new server, the agent registration on the old server may still be active and the prompt will not appear on the new server until that registration is properly handled.
steps_html: In VALC, open Manage Client → Client Details for the migrating customer.
Change Setup Step to "Created" and save.
Hard-refresh the browser on the new server (Ctrl+F5) and log in again with GSIADMIN credentials.
The agent download prompt should now appear.

scenarios.title: Agent install hangs at "Validating Data"
scenarios.subtitle: database connectivity blocked at install time.
scenarios.data_search: agent install hangs at validating data database connectivity blocked at install time during agent install the browser displays validating data indefinitely instead of progressing to the sql server connection prompt or deploying the agent can t reach the database server either dns firewall credentials or the database itself is the blocker verify each connection layer in turn before escalating during agent install the browser displays validating data indefinitely instead of progressing to the sql server connection prompt or deploying install verify database connectivity from the application server then escalate to dba agent install reaches validating data but never advances the most common root cause family is the application server can t reach the database server bad sql port blocked firewall missing database or invalid credentials from the application server attempt a manual connection to the database server on the configured port to verify reachability confirm the database the rruser login and sql authentication mode are all in place per the pre-install database setup if the prompt is reached but still fails double-check the values being entered match the customer s sql configuration exactly check windows event viewer on the application server for rapidreconciler-related errors check today s agent out and err log files see diagnostic tools if the install needs to be restarted ensure setup step is set back to created in valc before retrying

solutions.title: Verify database connectivity from the application server, then escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: Agent install reaches "Validating Data" but never advances. The most common root cause family is the application server can't reach the database server — bad SQL port, blocked firewall, missing database, or invalid credentials.
steps_html: From the application server, attempt a manual connection to the database server on the configured port to verify reachability.
Confirm the database, the rruser login, and SQL Authentication mode are all in place per the pre-install database setup.
If the prompt is reached but still fails, double-check the values being entered match the customer's SQL configuration exactly.
Check Windows Event Viewer on the application server for RapidReconciler-related errors.
Check today's agent .out and .err log files (see Diagnostic Tools).
If the install needs to be restarted, ensure Setup Step is set back to "Created" in VALC before retrying.

scenarios.title: Agent services not running on application server
scenarios.subtitle: Windows Services console shows Stopped or service won't start.
scenarios.data_search: agent services not running on application server windows services console shows stopped or service won t start in windows services console rapidreconciler agent or rapidreconciler services is stopped or fails to start common causes are a windows update reboot expired service-account credentials a corrupted config file or a stopped dependency service walk the service-state checks this often resolves with a service restart in windows services console rapidreconciler agent or rapidreconciler services is shown as stopped or fails to start application server verify service state dependencies and credentials windows services console shows rr agent or rr services as stopped or failing to start walk through restart dependencies account credentials and config-file integrity on the application server open services console services msc right-click rapidreconciler agent and click start repeat for rapidreconciler services if the service fails to start check today s agent wrapper log file for the startup error verify the service s log-on credentials are still valid check windows event viewer application log for related errors at the time of failure if the wrapper log indicates a config issue escalate to a senior tech to investigate the agent installation

solutions.title: Verify service state, dependencies, and credentials
solutions.when_applies_html: Windows Services console shows RR Agent or RR Services as Stopped or failing to start. Walk through restart, dependencies, account credentials, and config-file integrity.
steps_html: On the application server, open Services console (services.msc).
Right-click "RapidReconciler Agent" and click Start. Repeat for "RapidReconciler Services".
If the service fails to start, check today's agent .wrapper log file for the startup error.
Verify the service's log-on credentials are still valid.
Check Windows Event Viewer (Application log) for related errors at the time of failure.
If the wrapper log indicates a config issue, escalate to a senior tech to investigate the agent installation.

scenarios.title: Browser shows "Not Secure" or certificate warning
scenarios.subtitle: wildcard cert expired or renewed cert not yet deployed.
scenarios.data_search: browser shows not secure or certificate warning wildcard cert expired or renewed cert not yet deployed when a customer navigates to their rapidreconciler url the browser shows a security warning or not secure indicator almost always a cert renewal deployment issue either the cert expired or the renewed cert hasn t been deployed to the customer s agent or backend escalate to ui developer network tech for the renewal pipeline when a customer navigates to their rapidreconciler url the browser shows a security warning or not secure indicator certificate dns verify cert expiry and deployment state escalate to renewal pipeline customer s browser shows not secure or a certificate warning when reaching the rr url walk through the renewal pipeline state cert expiry agent deployment backend configuration and escalate to whoever owns the layer that hasn t been updated check the certificate expiry date in the browser by clicking the lock warning icon in the address bar if expired or close to expiry verify with gsi that the wildcard getgsi com cert renewal is complete if renewal is complete but deployment hasn t happened redeploy the agent for this customer via valc manage deploys verify in the browser that the new certificate is active after redeployment for systemic issues across many customers confirm the ui developer has updated the backend configuration

solutions.title: Verify cert expiry and deployment state; escalate to renewal pipeline
solutions.when_applies_html: Customer's browser shows "Not Secure" or a certificate warning when reaching the RR URL. Walk through the renewal pipeline state — cert expiry, agent deployment, backend configuration — and escalate to whoever owns the layer that hasn't been updated.
steps_html: Check the certificate expiry date in the browser by clicking the lock/warning icon in the address bar.
If expired or close to expiry, verify with GSI that the wildcard *.getgsi.com cert renewal is complete.
If renewal is complete but deployment hasn't happened, redeploy the agent for this customer via VALC Manage Deploys.
Verify in the browser that the new certificate is active after redeployment.
For systemic issues across many customers, confirm the UI Developer has updated the backend configuration.

scenarios.title: Clients grid row not updating to "Connected" after install
scenarios.subtitle: install reported complete, but VALC still shows the old status.
scenarios.data_search: clients grid row not updating to connected after install install reported complete but valc still shows the old status agent install reported installation complete but on the valc clients grid the row s status badge stays at the previous value instead of switching to connected the install ran but valc isn t seeing a heartbeat check dns propagation services and agent external ip in turn agent install reported installation complete but on the valc clients grid the row s status badge stays at the previous value instead of switching to connected install verify dns services and agent external ip agent install reported installation complete but valc still shows the old status walk down the layers dns propagation then rr agent rr services then the external ip recorded on client details refresh the clients grid in your browser the page does not auto-refresh wait a few minutes if the install just completed from the customer s application server try resolving the customer s getgsi com url if dns still returns the old ip the cloudflare update hasn t propagated yet on the application server confirm both rapidreconciler agent and rapidreconciler services are running in the windows services console verify the agent external ip address on the client details tab matches the customer s actual external ip if the row still hasn t updated check today s agent out log file for connection errors back to valc

solutions.title: Verify DNS, services, and Agent External IP
solutions.when_applies_html: Agent install reported "Installation Complete" but VALC still shows the old status. Walk down the layers: DNS propagation, then RR Agent / RR Services, then the External IP recorded on Client Details.
steps_html: Refresh the Clients grid in your browser (the page does not auto-refresh).
Wait a few minutes if the install just completed.
From the customer's application server, try resolving the customer's .getgsi.com URL — if DNS still returns the old IP, the Cloudflare update hasn't propagated yet.
On the application server, confirm both "RapidReconciler Agent" and "RapidReconciler Services" are running in the Windows Services console.
Verify the Agent External IP Address on the Client Details tab matches the customer's actual external IP.
If the row still hasn't updated, check today's agent .out log file for connection errors back to VALC.

scenarios.title: Companies tab is empty after install
scenarios.subtitle: database is Online but no companies populated.
scenarios.data_search: companies tab is empty after install database is online but no companies populated database shows online on the databases tab but selecting it from the dropdown on the companies tab shows no company numbers to license companies populate from jde through the ssis package s first run if sql agent isn t running or the ssis source connection is misconfigured or jde has no companies in the source tables the list stays empty database shows online on the databases tab but selecting it from the dropdown on the companies tab shows no company numbers to license database ssis verify the ssis first-run pipeline escalate to dba database is online but the companies tab shows nothing in the dropdown the companies come from jde through ssis check sql agent the ssis source connection to jde and jde s source tables on the companies tab check the last company sync timestamp at the top-right if it shows recent activity give the sync a few more minutes and refresh the page on the database server verify the rapidreconciler sql agent job is enabled and has run successfully at least once check the sql agent job history for errors on the most recent run if the job is failing or has never run escalate to the gsi database administrator to investigate the ssis package and jde connection

solutions.title: Verify the SSIS first-run pipeline; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: Database is Online but the Companies tab shows nothing in the dropdown. The companies come from JDE through SSIS — check SQL Agent, the SSIS source connection to JDE, and JDE's source tables.
steps_html: On the Companies tab, check the "Last Company Sync" timestamp at the top-right. If it shows recent activity, give the sync a few more minutes and refresh the page.
On the database server, verify the RapidReconciler SQL Agent job is enabled and has run successfully at least once.
Check the SQL Agent job history for errors on the most recent run.
If the job is failing or has never run, escalate to the GSI database administrator to investigate the SSIS package and JDE connection.

solutions.title: Verify the customer is on VPN or the corporate network
solutions.when_applies_html: The customer is working remotely (home, travel, off-site). RapidReconciler's backend is only reachable from the customer's network or via VPN; if they're disconnected, login succeeds but no data flows. This is the single most common cause for a remote worker.
steps_html: Ask the customer if they are working remotely (home / travel / off-site).
If remote, ask them to confirm they are connected via VPN or their organization's approved remote-access method.
If they are not connected, ask them to connect to VPN and try again.
If they believe they are connected, ask them to disconnect and reconnect the VPN, then hard-refresh the browser.
If the issue persists after confirmed VPN connection, proceed to the next customer-side check (hard reload).

solutions.title: Hard reload the browser
solutions.when_applies_html: The customer is confirmed on the corporate network or VPN. Stale browser cache or a partially-failed initial page load is the most common simple cause; the agent script may need to re-authenticate after a transient connectivity blip.
steps_html: Ask the customer to perform a hard reload of the browser using Shift + F5 (Windows) or Cmd + Shift + R (Mac).
If that doesn't resolve it, ask them to fully close all browser windows and reopen, then log in again.
If still no data appears, proceed to the browser local network access check.

solutions.title: Enable browser local-network access permission
solutions.when_applies_html: The customer's browser was recently updated, or this is a new workstation. Edge and Chrome introduced a permission gate that controls whether sites can connect to local-network devices — if RapidReconciler isn't on the allowed list for that customer's machine, all data calls silently fail.
steps_html: Walk the customer through Edge: Settings → Privacy, search, and services → Site permissions → All permissions → Local network access.
In the "Customized behaviors" section, check the "Allowed to connect to any device on your local network" list.
Verify rapidreconciler.getgsi.com is present in that allowed list.
If missing, click "Add site" next to that list and add rapidreconciler.getgsi.com.
Chrome has an equivalent setting under its site-permissions area — wording differs slightly but the concept is the same.
After adding the site, hard-reload the page and verify data appears.
If the customer cannot find or change the setting on their machine, escalate to their I/T department.

solutions.title: Clear browser data — last-resort browser cleanup
solutions.when_applies_html: VPN confirmed, hard reload didn't help, local network access is correctly configured, and Customer I/T has confirmed no firewall changes. Stale or corrupted authentication tokens, cookies, or service-worker state can hold the page in a broken state.
steps_html: Refer the customer to their I/T department for browser-specific instructions on clearing browser data.
The general approach is to clear cookies and cached files for rapidreconciler.getgsi.com (not all sites), then close the browser fully and reopen.
After clearing, have the customer log in again with a fresh session.

If the issue still persists after all customer-side checks, the issue is on the GSI side — proceed to the agent-side scenarios in this document and check the agent connectivity from the customer's server.
If exhausted all checks and no resolution, email rrsupport@getgsi.com with the customer details and the steps already attempted.

solutions.title: Check the browser DevTools console for errors
solutions.when_applies_html: All customer-side basic checks come back clean and the Database tab in VALC shows healthy. Opening the customer's browser DevTools Console is the next step to see whether the page is silently failing — JS errors, blocked requests, CORS rejections, or the agent script not loading at all.
steps_html: Have the customer open DevTools: in Edge or Chrome press F12 (or Ctrl+Shift+I), then click the Console tab.
Ask the customer to clear the console (the ⊘ icon at top left), then have them perform the action that fails — usually login, then navigating to the page where data should appear.

Compare against the healthy-baseline pattern. A working session shows lines like URL visited /page/login and URL visited /app/inventory/reconciliation from base.js — that confirms the agent is loaded and the customer is navigating the app.

Ignore the known noise: warnings about 'popover' attribute with an invalid value are AngularJS template noise from the UI and are harmless. 404s on fontawesome-webfont.woff or Simple-Line-Icons.woff are cosmetic font fallbacks and do not affect data flow.

Look for the actual signal: any red error from base.js with a stack trace, any 4xx/5xx on the application's own API calls (not the .woff files), CORS or Mixed Content warnings, net::ERR_BLOCKED_BY_CLIENT entries from a browser extension, or the complete absence of URL visited lines from base.js.
Have the customer screenshot or copy the relevant errors and email them to rrsupport@getgsi.com. Right-click in the console → Save as… exports the full log if a longer trace is needed.
If the console shows no base.js activity at all, the agent script never loaded — jump to Agent services are not running and Browser shows “Not Secure”.
If the console shows red errors on application API calls (4xx/5xx), the agent loaded but the back end is unhappy — check SQL Agent job not running and the database scenarios.

If the console shows net::ERR_BLOCKED_BY_CLIENT or CORS blocks, the customer's browser environment is interfering — ad-blocker, corporate DLP, or proxy. Have them retry in an incognito window with extensions disabled to confirm.

solutions.title: Investigate recent customer-side network or security changes
solutions.when_applies_html: Sudden onset (started today, no apparent customer-side change), multiple users at the same customer affected simultaneously, OR the admin reports a "No database connection" message specifically on Admin > General. The customer's IT team has likely rolled out a firewall change, DNS filter, web content filter, secure web gateway, or endpoint security update.
steps_html: Acknowledge the customer's theory about the new company, then explain that the "No Database Connection" message means traffic to the database is being blocked between their workstation/server and GSI — which a RapidReconciler config change cannot cause. This refocuses the conversation on the network path.

Confirm the message location is Admin > General (not just "no data on the inventory grid"). If it is on Admin > General specifically, that strongly points to this scenario over the generic data-display scenarios.

Ask the customer: "Has your I/T team recently turned on, updated, or changed the policy on any web filtering, DNS filtering, secure web gateway, or endpoint security product?" Common products to ask about by name so the customer can recognize them:
  • Web / DNS filters & secure web gateways: Cisco Umbrella, Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA), Cloudflare Gateway, Forcepoint Web Security, Barracuda Web Security Gateway, iboss, DNSFilter, TitanHQ WebTitan, Symantec / Broadcom Cloud SWG, Netskope
  • Next-gen firewalls with URL filtering: Palo Alto Networks (incl. Prisma Access), Fortinet FortiGate / FortiGuard, SonicWall, Check Point, Sophos Firewall / Web Appliance, Cato Networks
  • Endpoint security / EDR with web blocking: CrowdStrike Falcon, SentinelOne, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint / Defender SmartScreen, Sophos Endpoint, Trend Micro Apex One

Also ask whether anything changed even if the customer doesn't think it's related: a Windows Update / patch rollout, a new VPN client, a new corporate root certificate push, or a switch from split-tunnel to full-tunnel VPN.

Ask the customer to test from a different machine on the same network and from a machine on a different network (e.g. mobile hotspot) — if the issue follows the network and not the user, that further confirms a customer-network filter.

Have the customer's I/T team explicitly whitelist rapidreconciler.getgsi.com (and any related GSI hostnames/IPs the network team has on file) on the product they identified, then have the customer hard-refresh the browser and re-check Admin > General.
If Customer I/T confirms no recent changes and no filter is in scope, escalate to Tier 2 (RR Network Tech) and proceed to the agent-side / database connectivity scenarios in this document.

If the issue persists after Customer I/T whitelisting and a hard refresh, email rrsupport@getgsi.com with the customer details, the products checked with Customer I/T, and the steps already attempted.

solutions.title: Per-customer hostname fails to resolve (escalate to Network Tech)
solutions.when_applies_html: DevTools Console shows red net::ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED errors on requests to a per-customer RapidReconciler hostname (e.g. rrprod-<customercode>.getgsi.com). The login page may load fine but data fetches fail because the front-end can't reach the customer-specific backend.
steps_html: Confirm the failing URL pattern in the customer's DevTools Console. Note the exact per-customer hostname and port (e.g. rrprod-<customercode>.getgsi.com:NNNNN). Capture a screenshot of the console so the network signature is preserved if the issue is intermittent.

Run the cross-network triage. Have the customer test from another machine on the same network, then from a different network (mobile hotspot or personal phone tether is fine). This is the single highest-value test — about two minutes and it tells you which side owns the problem.

Interpret the test:
  • Same-network machine fails the same way + different network works → customer-side. Their corporate network is blocking or failing to resolve the hostname.
  • Same-network machine fails the same way + different network also fails → GSI-side. The DNS record is broken or missing on GSI's authoritative DNS.
  • Same-network machine works + this user's machine fails → user-machine local. Stale DNS cache, hosts file, or browser profile.

Check whether multiple users at the same customer are reporting identical errors right now. If yes, treat as GSI-side until proven otherwise (a customer-network issue typically wouldn't cause every user at the customer to fail simultaneously).

If the verdict is customer-side: have the customer's I/T explicitly whitelist the per-customer hostname (and the port) on their DNS filter, web filter, and firewall. Ask whether they recently rolled out a DNS-filtering product (Cisco Umbrella, Zscaler, Cloudflare Gateway, DNSFilter, etc.) or changed VPN config (split- vs. full-tunnel). After whitelisting, have the customer hard-refresh and recheck the console.

If the verdict is GSI-side: escalate to GSI I/T (Network Tech) to verify the A record for the per-customer hostname on GSI's authoritative DNS, and to confirm the per-customer backend host is up. Do not have the customer keep retrying — the issue is not on their end.

If the verdict is user-machine local: have the user run ipconfig /flushdns (Windows) or sudo dscacheutil -flushcache (Mac), check their hosts file (C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts on Windows; /etc/hosts on Mac/Linux) for a stale entry containing the GSI hostname, and retry. If still failing, have them try in a private/incognito window and a different browser to rule out browser-profile DNS-over-HTTPS misconfiguration.
Once resolved, note in the ticket which path resolved it (customer I/T whitelist, GSI DNS fix, local DNS flush). This helps the next person when the same pattern surfaces from a different customer.

solutions.title: Cloudflare update made but data not appearing (escalate to Network Tech)
solutions.when_applies_html: Cloudflare has been updated by GSI I/T (Daren) for this customer and confirmed propagated, but data still doesn't appear in the customer's RapidReconciler UI. Most common during or just after a server migration where SQL Agent / SSIS / DNS were intentionally staged.
steps_html: On the database server, confirm the SQL Agent job schedule is enabled.
Trigger a one-time manual run of the SQL Agent job to validate it works.
Check the job history for errors on the most recent run.
Have the customer log out and log back in, or hard-refresh their browser.
If the issue is post-migration, double-check SSIS package connection strings on the new server.

scenarios.title: Customer's domain URL does not resolve
scenarios.subtitle: DNS A record needs attention.
scenarios.data_search: customer s domain url does not resolve dns a record needs attention customer cannot reach their rapidreconciler url at all the browser returns dns probe finished nxdomain or a similar resolution failure customer cannot reach their rapidreconciler url the domain returns dns probe finished nxdomain this site can t be reached or a similar dns resolution failure certificate dns dns a record is missing or incorrect a record was never created for this customer a record points to a wrong ip after a server change verify the url and escalate to network tech to fix dns a record any time the customer reports the url itself fails to resolve this is distinct from i logged in but see no data here the page never loads at all confirm the url the customer is using matches what s recorded in valc before escalating in valc open manage client client details and confirm the domain url value look up the a record for this domain e g via dig or nslookup and verify it returns the customer s correct internal ip if the record is missing or wrong contact gsi i t daren to verify and correct the dns entry after correction allow time for dns propagation before testing again

solutions.title: Verify the URL and escalate to Network Tech to fix DNS A record
solutions.when_applies_html: Any time the customer reports the URL itself fails to resolve. This is distinct from "I logged in but see no data" — here the page never loads at all. Confirm the URL the customer is using matches what's recorded in VALC before escalating.
steps_html: In VALC, open Manage Client → Client Details and confirm the Domain URL value.
Look up the A record for this domain (e.g. via dig or nslookup) and verify it returns the customer's correct internal IP.
If the record is missing or wrong, contact GSI I/T (Daren) to verify and correct the DNS entry.
After correction, allow time for DNS propagation before testing again.

scenarios.title: DNS propagation taking longer than expected
scenarios.subtitle: GSI made the DNS update; customer still doesn't see it.
scenarios.data_search: dns propagation taking longer than expected gsi made the dns update customer still doesn t see it a dns update was made by gsi i t daren several hours ago but the change is still not visible to the customer usually just a propagation cache issue isp ttls customer s local dns cache or an intermediate caching resolver path 2 tell the customer to wait optionally help them flush their cache a dns update was made by gsi i t daren several hours ago but the change is still not visible to the customer certificate dns verify update was made walk the customer through cache flushing suggest waiting gsi i t daren made the dns update some hours ago but the customer still can t reach the site confirm the update was actually made on the gsi side then it s a customer-side caching question allow additional time propagation can take up to 24 hours depending on ttl settings have the customer flush their local dns cache ipconfig flushdns on windows if the issue persists beyond 24 hours contact gsi i t daren to verify the dns change is visible at the authoritative nameserver test from a different network to rule out customer-side dns caching

solutions.title: Verify update was made, walk the customer through cache flushing, suggest waiting
solutions.when_applies_html: GSI I/T (Daren) made the DNS update some hours ago but the customer still can't reach the site. Confirm the update was actually made on the GSI side, then it's a customer-side caching question.
steps_html: Allow additional time — propagation can take up to 24 hours depending on TTL settings.
Have the customer flush their local DNS cache (ipconfig /flushdns on Windows).
If the issue persists beyond 24 hours, contact GSI I/T (Daren) to verify the DNS change is visible at the authoritative nameserver.
Test from a different network to rule out customer-side DNS caching.

scenarios.title: Daily export fails — "restart service" is a temporary fix
scenarios.subtitle: works some days, not others; restart helps for ~24 hours.
scenarios.data_search: daily export fails restart service is a temporary fix works some days not others restart helps for 24 hours customer reports the end-of-day export works some days and not others restarting the rapidreconciler service on the app server is a workaround that lasts about 24 hours the restart fixes it for a day pattern is the diagnostic gold recommend a full app-server reboot for a more durable fix customer reports the end-of-day export works some days and not others they have already discovered that clicking restart service on the rr app server resolves it temporarily but it stops working again within 24 hours application server export job customer phrases it as the export sometimes doesn t work or we ve been restarting the service every day they may have asked their internal it to handle the daily restart but want a more permanent fix recommend a full application-server reboot the restart service is a temporary fix pattern is the diagnostic gold it indicates the underlying service is in a bad state that a full reboot will clear the customer s own it can execute the reboot once you recommend it recognize the restart service is a temporary fix pattern in the customer s description this is the key signal reply to the customer recommending a full application-server reboot rather than the daily service restart frame it as the service restart is treating the symptom a full reboot should clear the underlying state and give a more durable fix note that this requires their it to schedule a brief outage on the app server suggest off-hours timing ask them to confirm when the reboot is scheduled then to update the ticket after it s been done follow up after 48 hours to confirm the export is now stable if the issue recurs after a full reboot escalate to rr network tech the workaround restart pattern means there s a deeper issue worth investigating

solutions.title: Recommend a full application-server reboot
solutions.when_applies_html: The "restart service is a temporary fix" pattern is the diagnostic gold — it indicates the underlying service is in a bad state that a full reboot will clear. The customer's own IT can execute the reboot once you recommend it.
steps_html: Recognize the "restart service is a temporary fix" pattern in the customer's description — this is the key signal.

Reply to the customer recommending a full application-server reboot rather than the daily service restart. Frame it as "the service restart is treating the symptom; a full reboot should clear the underlying state and give a more durable fix."
Note that this requires their IT to schedule a brief outage on the app server. Suggest off-hours timing.
Ask them to confirm when the reboot is scheduled, then to update the ticket after it's been done.

Follow up after 48 hours to confirm the export is now stable. If the issue recurs after a full reboot, escalate to RR Network Tech — the workaround/restart pattern means there's a deeper issue worth investigating.

scenarios.title: Database status shows "Unknown" or "Stopped"
scenarios.subtitle: VALC Databases tab shows orange instead of green.
scenarios.data_search: database status shows unknown or stopped valc databases tab shows orange instead of green on the databases tab a row shows unknown orange or stopped orange instead of online green the connection between valc and the customer s sql server is broken could be credentials the agent service the firewall the database itself or sql authentication settings verify each layer usually requires dba escalation on the databases tab a row shows unknown orange or stopped orange instead of online green database valc verify each connection layer escalate to dba valc databases tab shows unknown or stopped on a database row walk through the connection layers in order credentials agent service firewall database existence sql auth settings and escalate to dba with what you ruled out open the edit database popup for the failing row in valc and verify every field matches the actual database configuration confirm the rr agent service is running on the customer s application server if not start it check today s agent out log file on the application server for connection errors or stack traces from the application server attempt a manual connection to the database server on the configured port e g telnet 10 x x x 1433 to confirm reachability confirm the customer s dba has created the rapidreconciler prod database and the rruser sql authentication login if everything looks correct and status remains failed escalate to the gsi database administrator

solutions.title: Verify each connection layer; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: VALC Databases tab shows Unknown or Stopped on a database row. Walk through the connection layers in order — credentials, agent service, firewall, database existence, SQL Auth settings — and escalate to DBA with what you ruled out.
steps_html: Open the Edit Database popup for the failing row in VALC and verify every field matches the actual database configuration.
Confirm the RR Agent service is running on the customer's application server. If not, start it.
Check today's agent .out log file on the application server for connection errors or stack traces.
From the application server, attempt a manual connection to the database server on the configured port (e.g., telnet 10.x.x.x 1433) to confirm reachability.
Confirm the customer's DBA has created the RapidReconciler_Prod database and the rruser SQL Authentication login.
If everything looks correct and status remains failed, escalate to the GSI database administrator.

solutions.title: Walk the variance investigation, then escalate the Excel if needed
solutions.when_applies_html: Customer reports a red Inventory Validation light or "out of balance" / variance message. Common phrasings: "the red light came on," "this just popped up," "we have a $X variance for [period]." First check is unposted GL batches in the prior period.
steps_html: Acknowledge the variance with the customer. Note the period (e.g. April 2024) and the variance amount. Ask for a screenshot of the Reconciliation page including the Variance Calculation panel if not already attached.

Read the indicator’s own tooltip back to the customer. Hovering over the red Inventory Validation indicator shows a popup that says: “This indicator is red if the roll forward from the prior period fails. This is most likely due to one or more un-posted GL batches. If all batches in the prior period have been posted, please contact us at rrsupport@getgsi.com for assistance.” Many customers don’t notice this message — pointing them at it sometimes resolves the case before any further troubleshooting.

Ask the customer to verify whether all GL batches in the prior period have been posted. This is a customer-side check in their JDE/ERP, not in RapidReconciler. The product’s own tooltip says this is the most likely cause and is correct in our experience.

If unposted batches are found: have the customer post the batches, then refresh the Reconciliation page and confirm the Inventory Validation indicator goes green. If green, resolved — close the loop with the customer. Roughly 80% of cases end here.

If the customer confirms all prior-period batches are already posted: ask them to click the red Inventory Validation indicator. This exports an Excel report of the carry-forward calculation. Have them email the Excel to rrsupport@getgsi.com (or attach it to the existing support thread).

Escalate to Network Tech (Tier 2). Do not analyze the Excel at Tier 1 — the analysis is product/data work and a Network Tech is the right first read on whether to forward to RR Developer (Tier 3) for transaction-level review. Use the Create JSM ticket button below; the customer’s email and the Excel attachment travel with the escalation. Network Tech routes onward as needed.

If the customer reports no obvious cause from their side either (no unposted batches, no recent backdated entries they can identify), escalate via the same path. The Excel is still the artifact the next-tier tech needs — ask the customer to send it regardless.

something like connection to backend timed out customer has already tested from a different network mobile hotspot before contacting support confirm cross-network result then escalate to network tech login fails with a backend connect timeout error if the customer has already cross-network tested and confirmed it fails from outside their network too the issue is gsi-side backend availability or nsg rule don t have the customer keep retrying confirm the customer s exact error wording and the backend hostname shown in the error banner a screenshot of the login error is high-value to forward verify whether the customer has already done a cross-network test logged in from a different network e g mobile hotspot if yes that strongly indicates gsi-side capture the result reply to the customer availability fix an nsg rule change or a wider infrastructure issue

solutions.title: Confirm cross-network result, then escalate to Network Tech
solutions.when_applies_html: Login fails with a backend connect timeout error. If the customer has already cross-network tested and confirmed it fails from outside their network too, the issue is GSI-side (backend availability or NSG rule). Don't have the customer keep retrying.
steps_html: Confirm the customer's exact error wording and the backend hostname shown in the error banner. A screenshot of the login error is high-value to forward.
Verify whether the customer has already done a cross-network test (logged in from a different network, e.g. mobile hotspot). If yes, that strongly indicates GSI-side — capture the result.
Reply to the customer acknowledging the issue is on the GSI side and an internal escalation is in flight. Set realistic expectations on response time.
Compose the escalation email to RR Network Tech with: customer name, contact, backend hostname from the error, the cross-network test result, and the screenshot. Include the original ticket ID.
Diary the ticket for follow-up; note whether the resolution involved a backend availability fix, an NSG rule change, or a wider infrastructure issue.

scenarios.title: Migration complete but data not flowing on the new server
scenarios.subtitle: post-cutover data refresh hasn't kicked in.
scenarios.data_search: migration complete but data not flowing on the new server post-cutover data refresh hasn t kicked in after migration cutover the customer can reach the rapidreconciler ui on the new server but no new data appears several things were intentionally deferred during migration setup sql agent schedule ssis connection strings cloudflare a record any of which can be the blocker escalate to dba after walking the checklist after migration cutover the customer can reach the rapidreconciler ui on the new server but no new data appears migration walk the post-cutover checklist escalate to dba migration cutover happened but data doesn t flow on the new server walk the deferred-setup checklist sql agent schedule ssis connections cloudflare a record dns propagation and escalate to dba for the deeper ssis db layer on the new database server verify the sql agent job schedule is enabled if not enable it trigger a one-time manual run of the sql agent job to confirm it works on the new server open the ssis package on the new server and verify all connection strings point to the new server not the old one verify with gsi i t daren that the cloudflare a record has been updated to the new server s ip and propagation is complete have the customer hard-refresh their browser to clear any cached ui state

solutions.title: Walk the post-cutover checklist; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: Migration cutover happened but data doesn't flow on the new server. Walk the deferred-setup checklist (SQL Agent schedule, SSIS connections, Cloudflare A record, DNS propagation) and escalate to DBA for the deeper SSIS / DB layer.
steps_html: On the new database server, verify the SQL Agent job schedule is enabled. If not, enable it.
Trigger a one-time manual run of the SQL Agent job to confirm it works on the new server.
Open the SSIS package on the new server and verify all connection strings point to the new server, not the old one.
Verify with GSI I/T (Daren) that the Cloudflare A record has been updated to the new server's IP and propagation is complete.
Have the customer hard-refresh their browser to clear any cached UI state.

solutions.title: Recommend updating connection passwords (try first — cheap)
solutions.when_applies_html: First-pass response. The customer probably needs to update their connection passwords in the deployment-time connection configuration. This is correct in principle and resolves a fraction of cases — about 24-48 hours later you'll know if it stuck.
steps_html: Reply to the customer recommending they update their rruser connection passwords on the production deployment.

Frame it as "this is the most common cause — updating the connection passwords usually resolves it." Set expectations: if it doesn't fix it within 24 hours, schedule a Teams session for a deeper look.
Diary the ticket for 24-hour follow-up.
If the customer reports back that the import is still failing, proceed to Solution 2 (Teams session for the SSIS package internal connection).

solutions.title: Teams session: update the rruser Connection Manager INSIDE the SSIS package
solutions.when_applies_html: Solution 1 didn't resolve it. The customer updated deployment-time passwords but missed the Connection Manager INSIDE the SSIS package itself — that one is hardcoded into the package on deploy and needs Visual Studio to update.
steps_html: Schedule a 15-minute Teams session with the customer admin who has access to the SSIS package source.
On the call, have them open the SSIS package in Visual Studio.
In the Solution Explorer or Connection Managers panel, locate the Connection Manager that uses the rruser credential.
Right-click → Edit, re-enter the rruser password, and save.
Redeploy the package to the SSIS catalog.
Trigger the JDE import job manually to confirm it succeeds.
After the call, document which package and which connection manager needed the update — future deployments at the same customer may need the same fix.

scenarios.title: RapidReconciler data is stale — SQL Agent job not running
scenarios.subtitle: figures are old; the refresh pipeline isn't firing.
scenarios.data_search: rapidreconciler data is stale sql agent job not running figures are old the refresh pipeline isn t firing data in rapidreconciler is older than expected and isn t refreshing the ssis package was deployed correctly and previously ran fine but the sql agent job on the database server is stopped disabled failing or the schedule hasn t fired yet walk through the job state and history escalate to dba data in rapidreconciler is older than expected and isn t refreshing the customer reports yesterday s or older figures rather than current data even though the ssis package was deployed correctly and previously ran fine database sql agent verify sql agent job state and history escalate to dba customer reports yesterday s or older figures rather than current data even though the ssis package was deployed correctly and previously ran fine service-level checks is the sql server agent running is the job enabled are the recent runs succeeding on the database server open sql server configuration manager or services msc and confirm sql server agent is running if stopped start it if set to manual change to automatic so it survives the next reboot in ssms expand sql server agent jobs locate the rapidreconciler ssis job and confirm it is enabled right-click the job view history check the last several runs are they succeeding failing or not running at all a run that simply isn t happening points back to the agent service or the schedule runs that fail point to the package or its credentials if recent runs failed expand the failed run in history and read the step-level error message it identifies whether the failure is in the ssis package execution or in a downstream t-sql step if the job is healthy but stale data persists run the job manually right-click start job at step and watch it complete confirm fresh data lands in rapidreconciler prod verify the schedule on the job right-click properties schedules matches the agreed cadence with the customer

solutions.title: Verify SQL Agent job state and history; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: Customer reports yesterday's (or older) figures rather than current data, even though the SSIS package was deployed correctly and previously ran fine. Service-level checks — is the SQL Server Agent running? Is the job enabled? Are the recent runs succeeding?
steps_html: On the database server, open SQL Server Configuration Manager (or services.msc) and confirm SQL Server Agent is running. If stopped, start it; if set to Manual, change to Automatic so it survives the next reboot.
In SSMS, expand SQL Server Agent → Jobs, locate the RapidReconciler / SSIS job, and confirm it is Enabled.

Right-click the job → View History. Check the last several runs: are they succeeding, failing, or not running at all? A run that simply isn't happening points back to the Agent service or the schedule; runs that fail point to the package or its credentials.
If recent runs failed, expand the failed run in history and read the step-level error message — it identifies whether the failure is in the SSIS package execution or in a downstream T-SQL step.
If the job is healthy but stale data persists, run the job manually (right-click → Start Job at Step) and watch it complete. Confirm fresh data lands in RapidReconciler_Prod.
Verify the schedule on the job (right-click → Properties → Schedules) matches the agreed cadence with the customer.

scenarios.title: SSIS catalog deployment fails — permission denied
scenarios.subtitle: deploying account is missing privileges or SSISDB doesn't exist.
scenarios.data_search: ssis catalog deployment fails permission denied deploying account is missing privileges or ssisdb doesn t exist when deploying the ssis package to the ssisdb catalog deployment fails with a permission authentication error almost always one of three things the deploying account doesn t have sysadmin ssis admin windows authentication wasn t used or ssisdb hasn t been created escalate to dba when deploying the ssis package to the ssisdb catalog deployment fails with a permission authentication error database ssis deployment verify deployment account auth mode and ssisdb existence escalate to dba ssis catalog deployment fails with permission denied auth error three things to verify the deploying account s privileges the auth mode used and whether ssisdb itself exists on the target instance confirm you are deploying using windows authentication not sql authentication verify the deploying account has sysadmin or ssis admin privileges on the target sql server instance confirm the ssisdb catalog exists if not create it first if the sql agent job steps will run under a proxy account ensure the proxy has access to any unc paths used by the package

solutions.title: Verify deployment account, auth mode, and SSISDB existence; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: SSIS catalog deployment fails with permission denied / auth error. Three things to verify: the deploying account's privileges, the auth mode used, and whether SSISDB itself exists on the target instance.
steps_html: Confirm you are deploying using Windows Authentication, not SQL Authentication.
Verify the deploying account has sysadmin or ssis_admin privileges on the target SQL Server instance.
Confirm the SSISDB catalog exists — if not, create it first.
If the SQL Agent job steps will run under a proxy account, ensure the proxy has access to any UNC paths used by the package.

scenarios.title: SSIS package deployment fails — OLE DB driver error
scenarios.subtitle: OLE DB provider for JDE is missing or wrong version.
scenarios.data_search: ssis package deployment fails ole db driver error ole db provider for jde is missing or wrong version during ssis package deployment an error indicates the ole db provider for jde is not installed or cannot be found almost always a driver-installation issue on the database server either the wrong bitness 32 vs 64 the wrong version or not installed at all escalate to dba during ssis package deployment an error indicates the ole db provider for jde is not installed or cannot be found database ssis deployment verify ole db provider installation escalate to dba ssis package deployment fails with an ole db provider error usually means the jde ole db driver isn t installed or wrong bitness wrong version on the database server on the database server verify both 32-bit and 64-bit versions of the required ole db provider are installed re-install the ole db provider with both architectures if missing after installation re-run the ssis package deployment verify driver installation before the install meeting next time this is a known cause of install delays

solutions.title: Verify OLE DB provider installation; escalate to DBA
solutions.when_applies_html: SSIS package deployment fails with an OLE DB provider error. Usually means the JDE OLE DB driver isn't installed (or wrong bitness, wrong version) on the database server.
steps_html: On the database server, verify both 32-bit and 64-bit versions of the required OLE DB provider are installed.
Re-install the OLE DB provider with both architectures if missing.
After installation, re-run the SSIS package deployment.
Verify driver installation before the install meeting next time — this is a known cause of install delays.

scenarios.title: Setup Step not advancing automatically after install
scenarios.subtitle: Setup Step field stuck at "Created" or earlier.
scenarios.data_search: setup step not advancing automatically after install setup step field stuck at created or earlier after completing an install or migration the setup step field on the client details tab remains at created or an earlier value and does not advance either the install didn t finish cleanly or the setup step legitimately needs to be moved manually for this install path after completing an install or migration the setup step field on the client details tab remains at created or an earlier value and does not advance install verify install completed cleanly then advance setup step manually if needed setup step is not advancing automatically first confirm the install genuinely finished agent database connectivity services if everything checks out the state may need to be moved manually depending on the install path confirm the install actually completed end-to-end agent installed database showing online connectivity row showing connected if all of the above are good manually advance the setup step on the client details tab to the appropriate next value if you re not sure what value to set check another active client for reference save the change and refresh the clients grid to confirm the row updates

solutions.title: Verify install completed cleanly, then advance Setup Step manually if needed
solutions.when_applies_html: Setup Step is not advancing automatically. First, confirm the install genuinely finished (agent + database connectivity + services). If everything checks out, the state may need to be moved manually depending on the install path.
steps_html: Confirm the install actually completed end-to-end: agent installed, database showing Online, connectivity row showing Connected.
If all of the above are good, manually advance the Setup Step on the Client Details tab to the appropriate next value.
If you're not sure what value to set, check another active client for reference.
Save the change and refresh the Clients grid to confirm the row updates.

solutions.title: Collect the diagnostic Excel from the customer and escalate to Network Tech
solutions.when_applies_html: Customer reports the System Status light is red. Common phrasings: "system status is red," "the red light is on," "the job failed," "something crashed." This signals a backend job crash — the inbox person's job is to capture the Excel and hand off, not to diagnose.
steps_html: Acknowledge the alert with the customer. Note the date/time the indicator turned red, the company/business unit they were viewing, and ask for a screenshot of the Reconciliation page header (showing the System Status indicator) if not already attached.

Ask the customer to click the red System Status indicator. This exports an Excel report containing the crash details. Have them email the Excel to rrsupport@getgsi.com (or attach it to the existing support thread).

Once the Excel arrives, create a JSM ticket for the case and attach the Excel file to it. Do not analyze the Excel at Tier 1 — Tier 2 (Network Tech) is the first read on whether it’s a disk-space issue, a data error, or something else.

Escalate the JSM ticket to Tier 2 (Network Tech). Use the Create JSM ticket button below; the customer’s email and the Excel attachment travel with the escalation. Network Tech routes onward to RR Developer (Tier 3) if transaction-level review is needed.

If the customer cannot get the Excel to export (e.g., clicking the indicator does nothing), capture a screenshot of the indicator and any browser console error, and escalate without the Excel — note in the ticket that the export step failed so Tier 2 knows to start from the agent logs instead.

scenarios.title: UI loads but agent is not connecting
scenarios.subtitle: VALC shows the agent as Disconnected; UI accessible but no data.
scenarios.data_search: ui loads but agent is not connecting valc shows the agent as disconnected ui accessible but no data customer can reach the rapidreconciler ui at their domain url but the valc clients grid shows the agent as disconnected or no data appears in the ui usually an ip-change issue the customer s internal ip changed but valc and or dns still points at the old one escalate to network tech customer can reach the rapidreconciler ui at their domain url but the valc clients grid shows the agent as disconnected or no data appears in the ui certificate dns agent verify internal ip in valc and dns escalate to network tech ui is reachable but agent is disconnected on the clients grid almost always the customer s internal ip changed and either valc or the dns a record still points at the old ip walk through then escalate to network tech for ip dns update in valc verify the agent internal ip address on the client details tab matches the customer s actual current ip if different update the ip in valc submit a dns update request to gsi i t daren with the corrected internal ip on the application server verify the rr agent service is running after dns propagation confirm the clients grid row updates to connected

solutions.title: Verify Internal IP in VALC and DNS; escalate to Network Tech
solutions.when_applies_html: UI is reachable but agent is Disconnected on the Clients grid. Almost always: the customer's internal IP changed, and either VALC or the DNS A record still points at the old IP. Walk through, then escalate to Network Tech for IP/DNS update.
steps_html: In VALC, verify the Agent Internal IP Address on the Client Details tab matches the customer's actual current IP.
If different, update the IP in VALC.
Submit a DNS update request to GSI I/T (Daren) with the corrected internal IP.
On the application server, verify the RR Agent service is running.
After DNS propagation, confirm the Clients grid row updates to Connected.

scenarios.title: User sees no companies after admin granted access
scenarios.subtitle: session is holding stale security settings.
scenarios.data_search: user sees no companies after admin granted access session is holding stale security settings a user with newly-granted access clicks into a tab and sees no companies in the company selector permission was granted but their session cached the old empty permission set the fix is a refresh or re-login on their side this is the simplest scenario to resolve via email reply customer admin reports that a user with access to one or more companies clicks into a tab reconciliation inventory etc and sees no companies in the company selector the user is set to active the customer admin has confirmed in admin users that the right companies and tab columns are checked under authorized companies and they have saved the user from the gsi side everything in valc looks healthy network user permissions refresh the widget or have the user log out and back in customer admin reports a user with newly-granted authorized companies access can t see companies in the selector admin users shows the right companies tabs are checked and saved from the gsi side valc looks healthy the user s session is just holding the old permissions confirm with the customer admin that the user s authorized companies are saved correctly open admin users click the edit pencil icon for the user and check the authorized companies section confirm the right companies are ticked under the column that matches the tab the user is trying to use reconciliation has its own column inventory has its own etc if the boxes are unticked tick them and click save that s the fix stop here if the boxes are correctly ticked and saved the user s session is the issue have the user click the refresh icon in the top-right corner of the widget filter company-selector area on the page they re on this forces the widget to re-read security settings from the rr app and the missing companies should appear if the widget refresh doesn t surface the companies have the user log out and log back in a fresh login pulls the current security settings from the server and bypasses anything the browser had cached for the previous session if neither the widget refresh nor a re-login works have the user hard-refresh the browser ctrl f5 on windows cmd shift r on mac on the affected page then log in again this clears any cached page assets that might still be holding old permissions if after a clean re-login the user still sees no companies on the tab re-open the user record in admin users on the customer admin s side and verify one more time that the boxes were saved closing the authorized companies dialog with the x instead of save can drop the changes silently if none of the above resolves it capture a screenshot of the user s authorized companies dialog as the customer admin sees it along with a screenshot of what the user sees on the tab and email both to rrsupport getgsi com at that point the issue is more likely on the back end than session caching

solutions.title: Refresh the widget or have the user log out and back in
solutions.when_applies_html: Customer admin reports a user with newly-granted Authorized Companies access can't see companies in the selector. Admin > Users shows the right companies/tabs are checked and saved. From the GSI side, VALC looks healthy. The user's session is just holding the old permissions.
steps_html: Confirm with the customer admin that the user's Authorized Companies are saved correctly: open Admin → Users, click the edit (pencil) icon for the user, and check the Authorized Companies section. Confirm the right companies are ticked under the column that matches the tab the user is trying to use (Reconciliation has its own column, Inventory has its own, etc.). If the boxes are unticked, tick them and click Save — that's the fix; stop here.

If the boxes are correctly ticked and saved, the user's session is the issue. Have the user click the refresh icon in the top-right corner of the widget (filter / company-selector area) on the page they're on. This forces the widget to re-read security settings from the RR app and the missing companies should appear.

If the widget refresh doesn't surface the companies, have the user log out and log back in. A fresh login pulls the current security settings from the server and bypasses anything the browser had cached for the previous session.

If neither the widget refresh nor a re-login works, have the user hard-refresh the browser (Ctrl+F5 on Windows / Cmd+Shift+R on Mac) on the affected page, then log in again. This clears any cached page assets that might still be holding old permissions.

If after a clean re-login the user still sees no companies on the tab, re-open the user record in Admin → Users on the customer admin's side and verify one more time that the boxes were saved — closing the Authorized Companies dialog with the X instead of Save can drop the changes silently.

If none of the above resolves it, capture a screenshot of the user's Authorized Companies dialog (as the customer admin sees it) along with a screenshot of what the user sees on the tab, and email both to rrsupport@getgsi.com. At that point the issue is more likely on the back end than session caching.
